title: Met Office Digital: Nowcast Rainfall Intensity – SE Wales Hotspot
evidence: Met Office Digital nowcast data shows a persistent area of high rainfall intensity over Southeast Wales, centred approximately over the Monmouthshire and Blaenau Gwent areas. Rainfall rates of 15–25mm/hr recorded in the nowcast grid. High correlation with areas where Traffic Wales advisories have already been issued.

assessment.justification: Met Office Digital nowcast shows persistent high-intensity rainfall hotspot over SE Wales. Highly consistent with the geographic concentration of emerging road advisories. Proxy corroboration from a high-authority meteorological source.
assessment.synthesis: Met Office nowcast confirms persistent high-intensity rainfall hotspot centred on SE Wales. Provides authoritative meteorological corroboration for the geographic clustering of early-morning road impacts in Monmouthshire and adjacent areas.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Proxy meteorological data. High-authority source. Consistent with impact pattern.

title: Met Office Digital: Accumulated Rainfall – SE Wales Totals Exceed 60mm
evidence: Met Office Digital accumulated rainfall analysis shows totals exceeding 60mm in a 12-hour period across parts of Southeast Wales, centred on Monmouthshire and the heads of the valleys area. These totals are consistent with monthly average rainfall for November delivered in half a day. High correlation with active flood warnings and road closures.

assessment.justification: Met Office accumulated rainfall totals of 60mm+ in 12 hours over SE Wales are an authoritative proxy indicator for the observed hydrological response. These totals explain the rapid river rises, the road flooding, and the wastewater network stress being reported from multiple official sources.
assessment.synthesis: Met Office accumulated rainfall analysis confirms 60mm+ in 12 hours over SE Wales. This meteorological data provides an authoritative causal explanation for all observed infrastructure and community impacts across Southeast Wales and adjacent areas.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. High-authority meteorological data. Explains and corroborates all concurrent impact observations.
